lines.english: but in the end, he dies, stuck to it. ||1||
and has an affair with another woman.
He is like the parrot, who is pleased to see the simbal tree;
The home of the sinner is on fire.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਲਤ ਰਹੈ ਮਿਟਵੈ ਕਬ ਨਾਹਿ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jalat rahai mittavai kab naeh |1| rahaau |
lines.english: It keeps burning, and the fire cannot be extinguished. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕੀ ਭਗਤਿ ਨ ਦੇਖੈ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: har kee bhagat na dekhai jaae |
lines.english: He does not go to see where the Lord is being worshipped.
He forgets the Primal Lord God, and is caught in the cycle of reincarnation.
He abandons the Lord's Path, and takes the wrong path.
He throws away the Ambrosial Nectar, and gathers poison to eat. ||2||
She dances to the beat, exciting the breath of those who watch her.
wearing beautiful clothes, decorated and adorned.
He is like the prostitute, who comes to dance,
One who has good karma recorded on his forehead,

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਾ ਕੇ ਗਲੇ ਜਮ ਕਾ ਹੈ ਫਾਸ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: vaa ke gale jam kaa hai faas |3|
lines.english: But the noose of the Messenger of Death is around her neck. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਭਜਿ ਪਰਿ ਹੈ ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਸਰਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: so bhaj par hai gur kee saranaa |
lines.english: hurries to enter the Guru's Sanctuary.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੰਡਾ ਮਰਕਾ ਜਾਇ ਪੁਕਾਰੇ ॥ ਪੜੈ ਨਹੀ ਹਮ ਹੀ ਪਚਿ ਹਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: sanddaa marakaa jaae pukaare | parrai nahee ham hee pach haare |
lines.english: Sanda and Marka went and complained to Harnaakhash, "Your son does not read his lessons. We are tired of trying to teach him.
O Saints, this is the way to cross over to the other side. ||4||2||8||
Says Naam Dayv, consider this:
He chants the Lord's Name,

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮੁ ਕਹੈ ਕਰ ਤਾਲ ਬਜਾਵੈ ਚਟੀਆ ਸਭੈ ਬਿਗਾਰੇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: raam kahai kar taal bajaavai chatteea sabhai bigaare |1|
lines.english: He chants the Lord's Name, clapping his hands to keep the beat; he has spoiled all the other students. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਸੁਧਾ ਬਸਿ ਕੀਨੀ ਸਭ ਰਾਜੇ ਬਿਨਤੀ ਕਰੈ ਪਟਰਾਨੀ ॥
lines.translit: basudhaa bas keenee sabh raaje binatee karai pattaraanee |
lines.english: "Your father the king has conquered the whole world", said his mother the queen.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਿਰਦੈ ਹਰਿ ਜੀ ਕੋ ਸਿਮਰਨੁ ਧਰੈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: hiradai har jee ko simaran dharai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: and he has enshrined meditative remembrance of the Lord within his heart." ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਤੁ ਪ੍ਰਹਿਲਾਦੁ ਕਹਿਆ ਨਹੀ ਮਾਨੈ ਤਿਨਿ ਤਉ ਅਉਰੈ ਠਾਨੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: poot prahilaad kahiaa nahee maanai tin tau aaurai tthaanee |2|
lines.english: "O Prahlad my son, you do not obey him, so he has decided to deal with you in another way." ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਸਟ ਸਭਾ ਮਿਲਿ ਮੰਤਰ ਉਪਾਇਆ ਕਰਸਹ ਅਉਧ ਘਨੇਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: dusatt sabhaa mil mantar upaaeaa karasah aaudh ghaneree |
lines.english: The council of villians met and resolved to send Prahlaad into the life hereafter.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਿਰਿ ਤਰ ਜਲ ਜੁਆਲਾ ਭੈ ਰਾਖਿਓ ਰਾਜਾ ਰਾਮਿ ਮਾਇਆ ਫੇਰੀ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: gir tar jal juaalaa bhai raakhio raajaa raam maaeaa feree |3|
lines.english: Prahlaad was thrown off a mountain, into the water, and into a fire, but the Sovereign Lord God saved him, by changing the laws of nature. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਢਿ ਖੜਗੁ ਕਾਲੁ ਭੈ ਕੋਪਿਓ ਮੋਹਿ ਬਤਾਉ ਜੁ ਤੁਹਿ ਰਾਖੈ ॥
lines.translit: kaadt kharrag kaal bhai kopio mohi bataau ju tuhi raakhai |
lines.english: Harnaakhash thundered with rage and threatened to kill Prahlaad. "Tell me, who can save you?"

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੀਤ ਪੀਤਾਂਬਰ ਤ੍ਰਿਭਵਣ ਧਣੀ ਥੰਭ ਮਾਹਿ ਹਰਿ ਭਾਖੈ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: peet peetaanbar tribhavan dhanee thanbh maeh har bhaakhai |4|
lines.english: Prahlaad answered, "The Lord, the Master of the three worlds, is contained even in this pillar to which I am tied." ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਿ ਨਾਮਦੇਉ ਹਮ ਨਰਹਰਿ ਧਿਆਵਹ ਰਾਮੁ ਅਭੈ ਪਦ ਦਾਤਾ ॥੫॥੩॥੯॥
lines.translit: keh naamadeo ham narahar dhiaavah raam abhai pad daataa |5|3|9|
lines.english: Says Naam Dayv, I meditate on the Lord, the Man-lion, the Giver of fearless dignity. ||5||3||9||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਨਾਖਸੁ ਜਿਨਿ ਨਖਹ ਬਿਦਾਰਿਓ ਸੁਰਿ ਨਰ ਕੀਏ ਸਨਾਥਾ ॥
lines.translit: haranaakhas jin nakhah bidaario sur nar kee sanaathaa |
lines.english: The Lord who tore Harnaakhash apart with His nails proclaimed Himself the Lord of gods and men.
let me see the actions of your Lord." ||1||
The Sultan said, "Listen, Naam Dayv:
The Sultan arrested Naam Dayv,
"Bring this dead cow back to life.
and said, "Let me see your Beloved Lord." ||1||Pause||
Otherwise, I shall cut off your head here and now." ||2||
No one can bring the dead back to life. ||3||
Naam Dayv answered, "O king, how can this happen?
I cannot do anything by my own actions.
He incited an elephant to attack. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਿ ਹੈ ਰਾਮੁ ਹੋਇ ਹੈ ਸੋਇ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: kar hai raam hoe hai soe |4|
lines.english: Whatever the Lord does, that alone happens." ||4||
The arrogant king was enraged at this reply.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨ ਹਉ ਤੇਰਾ ਪੂੰਗੜਾ ਨ ਤੂ ਮੇਰੀ ਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: n hau teraa poongarraa na too meree maae |
lines.english: Naam Dayv answered, "I am not your son, and you are not my mother.

lines.gurmukhi: ਛੋਡਿ ਰਾਮੁ ਕੀ ਨ ਭਜਹਿ ਖੁਦਾਇ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: chhodd raam kee na bhajeh khudaae |6|
lines.english: And she said, "Why don't you abandon your Lord Raam, and worship his Lord Allah?" ||6||
Naam Dayv's mother began to cry,
but Naam Dayv was saved, protected by the Lord. ||8||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਿੰਡੁ ਪੜੈ ਤਉ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਨ ਗਾਇ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: pindd parrai tau har gun gaae |7|
lines.english: Even if my body dies, I will still sing the Glorious Praises of the Lord." ||7||
The elephant attacked him with his trunk,
The people pleaded with the king, "Hear our prayer, O king.
but this Hindu has trampled my honor." ||9||
The king said, "The Qazis and the Mullahs bow down to me,
